name: Graeae
description: The Graeae, also known as the Grey Sisters or the Old Women, were three sisters in Greek mythology who shared one eye and one tooth among them. They were daughters of Phorcys and Ceto, and thus sisters to other monstrous figures like the Gorgons. Their names were Deino (Dread), Enyo (Horror), and Pemphredo (Alarm). The Graeae were depicted as old women, often with wrinkled skin and white hair, symbolizing their advanced age and wisdom. Their shared eye and tooth made them vulnerable, as whoever possessed these items could control or manipulate them. They guarded the path to the Gorgons, and were known for their knowledge of secret paths and prophecies. Perseus famously tricked them into revealing the location of the Nymphs who possessed the winged sandals, cap of invisibility, and kibisis (a bag for carrying Medusa's head), essential items in his quest to slay Medusa. The Graeae's reliance on each other and their unique disability highlight themes of vulnerability and the power of shared resources in Greek mythology. Their existence served as a challenge for heroes and a reminder of the strange and often frightening creatures that inhabited the world beyond the familiar.
subtitle: The Grey Sisters who shared one eye.
appearance: The Graeae were depicted as very old women with wrinkled skin, white hair, and a hunched posture. Their most distinctive feature was their shared eye and tooth, which they passed between each other. They were often described as being grotesque and frightening in appearance.
habitat: They lived in a cave or other remote location, often near the path to the Gorgons, guarding the way.
behavior: The Graeae were generally solitary and suspicious, guarding their secrets jealously. They were easily tricked or manipulated if someone gained possession of their shared eye or tooth. They were also known to argue and bicker among themselves.
weaknesses: Loss of their eye or tooth

keyMyths.title: Perseus and the Graeae
keyMyths.description: Perseus needed information to find Medusa, and the Graeae knew the way to the nymphs who possessed the necessary items. He stole their eye and tooth, forcing them to reveal the location in exchange for their return.

sources.author: Apollodorus
sources.description: A comprehensive source of Greek mythology.
sources.text: A comprehensive source of Greek mythology.
